evals.name: Demo 30s — Instantly Lead Gen
evals.prompt: Write a 30-second IG Reel script about using Instantly.ai to send cold emails that actually get replies. Comment trigger CTA with keyword COLD. Lead magnet: free cold email template pack. Key stat: 47% open rate.

assertions.criteria: The output contains exactly 6 hook options presented in a table format before the script
The teleprompter script section contains ONLY clean speakable text — no brackets, no stage directions, no visual cues, no [SLAM], no (camera pan), no production notes inline
assertions.reason: Teleprompter must be clean for reading on camera

assertions.criteria: The script mentions specific metrics (47% open rate or similar specific numbers) rather than vague claims

assertions.reason: CTA keyword must appear in output

assertions.criteria: The production brief contains all 4 hook types: Spoken, Text overlay, Visual, and Audio
The production brief contains screen directions with timestamps
The script body has exactly 2 distinct points (Setup+Payoff each) for a 30-second video, not 1 and not 3+
The tone is peer-to-peer (successful friend sharing knowledge), NOT teacher-to-student or lecture-style. No phrases like 'today we'll learn' or 'let me teach you'
assertions.reason: Jay's voice is peer authority, not teacher
Must reference the tool by name

assertions.criteria: The teleprompter script when read aloud at 150 words/minute would fit within approximately 30 seconds (60-80 words for the body, not counting title/CTA metadata)
assertions.reason: Script must fit the specified duration

assertions.criteria: The CTA strategy section specifies the keyword COLD, describes a lead magnet (cold email template pack), and mentions DM auto-reply setup

evals.name: Tutorial 60s — GoHighLevel Automation Setup
evals.prompt: Write a 60-second TikTok script teaching people how to set up a basic lead capture automation in GoHighLevel. Follow CTA. Mention that we've helped 2700+ businesses set this up.

assertions.criteria: The output contains exactly 6 hook options presented in a table format
The teleprompter script contains ONLY clean speakable text with no brackets, stage directions, or production notes inline
The script uses a Tutorial body framework: Promise (what viewer will learn) → Key step(s) → Result → CTA
assertions.reason: Tutorial content type must use tutorial framework

assertions.criteria: The script contains a re-hook phrase at approximately the midpoint (around 30 seconds in) — a phrase like 'but here's where it gets interesting' or 'watch this' to re-engage attention
assertions.reason: 60s videos require a re-hook at midpoint

assertions.criteria: The script has exactly 2 main teaching points for a 60-second video

assertions.reason: Must reference the tool by name

assertions.criteria: The production brief mentions TikTok-specific pacing (fastest, casual, aggressive hooks, more pattern interrupt)
The CTA is a Follow CTA (follow @leadgenjay or similar), NOT a comment trigger or link in bio
The teleprompter script reads at approximately 150 words/minute and would take about 60 seconds to deliver (130-170 words for the body)
assertions.reason: Script must fit the specified duration

assertions.criteria: The production brief contains screen directions with timestamps that span the full 60 seconds
assertions.reason: Screen directions must cover full duration

assertions.criteria: The tone uses casual TikTok-appropriate language with contractions and conversational phrasing, not formal or corporate

evals.name: Hot Take 15s — Cold Email Is Dead
evals.prompt: Write a 15-second IG Reel hot take script: 'Cold email is dead in 2026.' Controversial angle — argue that traditional cold email blasting is dead but warm outreach through AI personalization is thriving. Comment trigger CTA with keyword WARM. Lead magnet: warm outreach playbook.

assertions.criteria: The output contains exactly 6 hook options in a table
The teleprompter script is very short — only 5-7 sentences maximum for a 15-second video, and contains NO production notes or visual directions inline
The script has exactly 1 main point (not 2 or 3) since it's a 15-second video
The script takes a contrarian/provocative angle — argues cold email blasting is dead while warm AI-personalized outreach works
The teleprompter script when read aloud at 150 words/minute would fit within approximately 15 seconds (30-40 words for the body)
The script does NOT contain a re-hook phrase (no midpoint re-hook needed for 15s)
The production brief all 4 hook types (Spoken, Text overlay, Visual, Audio) and has screen directions
assertions.reason: Production brief must be complete even for short videos

assertions.criteria: The hook is provocative and attention-grabbing, using a contrarian or shock structure
assertions.reason: Hot take needs a strong hook

evals.name: Demo 60s with Research — Claude Code Lead Scraping
evals.prompt: Write a 60-second IG Reel demo script showing how to use Claude Code + Apify to scrape 500 leads in 60 seconds. Comment trigger CTA with keyword LEADS. Lead magnet: the exact Claude Code skill file. Before writing, check scraped_ideas for any relevant content about Claude Code or Apify or lead generation.

assertions.criteria: The output contains exactly 6 hook options in a table
The teleprompter script contains ONLY clean speakable text with no brackets, stage directions, or production notes
The script uses a Demo body framework: Context → Impressive thing (live demo) → Proof (show results) → Payoff
assertions.reason: Demo content type must use demo framework

assertions.criteria: The script contains a re-hook phrase at the midpoint for the 60-second duration
The production brief includes screen directions that specifically mention screen recording of Claude Code and/or Apify dashboard
The script mentions specific numbers (500 leads, 60 seconds, or similar concrete metrics)
The teleprompter script reads at approximately 150 words/minute and would take about 60 seconds to deliver
The CTA strategy mentions the keyword LEADS, describes the Claude Code skill file as the lead magnet, and includes DM auto-reply details
The script follows the 3-Beat Rule for demos: Setup (what we're doing + why) → Magic (the impressive moment) → Result (proof it worked)
